channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO assassin believed to have left a message at the scene on bullet casings: report
item.description: Evidence found at the scene of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson assassination point to a possible message left behind by the killer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Associated Press’ top photos of 2024 from the US
item.description: In 2024, U.S. photographers captured glimpses of humanity, ranging from a deeply divisive presidential election, to hurricanes and fires that ravaged communities, to campus protests over the war between Israel and Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Mattise, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Police hunt for the gunman who killed UnitedHealthcare's boss in Manhattan
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Investigators are searching for clues that could help them identify the masked gunman who stalked and killed the leader of one of the largest U.S. health insurance companies on a Manhattan sidewalk, then disappeared into Central Park.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KAREN MATTHEWS and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Rockefeller Center Christmas tree lights up in New York City
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Fifty-thousand multi-colored lights were illuminated on Rockefeller Center's iconic Christmas tree Wednesday evening to cheers from the crowds gathered to witness the annual New York City tradition.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bitcoin tops $100,000 as big rally sparked by Trump election win rolls on
item.description: Bitcoin has topped the $100,000 mark as a massive rally in the world’s most popular cryptocurrency sparked by the election of Donald Trump rolls on.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump nominates cryptocurrency advocate Paul Atkins as SEC chair
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump announced Wednesday that he intends to nominate cryptocurrency advocate Paul Atkins to chair the Securities and Exchange Commission. Trump said Atkins, the CEO of Patomak Partners and a former SEC commissioner, was a “proven leader for common sense regulations.
item.dc:creator: ALEX VEIGA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Taylor Swift 'Eras' tie-in book is a smash, selling more than 800,000 copies in first weekend
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just one retail chain was enough for Taylor Swift to have the top-selling book last week and the biggest publishing launch of 2024. According to Circana, Swift's “Eras Tour Book” sold 814,000 copies over the Thanksgiving holiday weekend.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police hunt for UnitedHealthcare CEO's masked killer after 'brazen, targeted' attack on NYC street
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A gunman killed UnitedHealthcare’s CEO on Wednesday in a “brazen, targeted attack” outside a Manhattan hotel where the health insurer was holding its investor conference, police said, setting off a massive search for the fleeing assailant hours before the annual Rockefeller Center Ch
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KAREN MATTHEWS and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Peter Navarro served prison time related to Jan. 6. Now Trump is bringing him back as an adviser
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former White House adviser Peter Navarro, who served prison time related to the Jan. 6 attack on the U.S. Capitol, will return to serve in Donald Trump’s second administration, the president-elect announced Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Opposition party leader arrested as Georgia authorities try to curb protests over ending EU talks
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — Georgian police on Wednesday raided the offices of an opposition party and arrested its leader in an apparent attempt to squelch a wave of mass protests triggered by the governing party’s decision to suspend negotiations on joining the European Union.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIKO MEGRELIDZE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: New LAPD chief says he will work to protect immigrants ahead of Trump's plans for mass deportations
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The new chief of the Los Angeles police force said his department is working with consulate offices for Mexico and other Central American countries to draft a plan to protect immigrants ahead of the Trump administration’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump names Billy Long as IRS head
item.description: Trump said his relationship with Long, who currently works as a business and tax adviser, dates back to 2011.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge considers first lawsuit to overturn Missouri's near-total abortion ban
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Abortion-rights advocates on Wednesday asked a judge to overturn Missouri’s near-total ban on the procedure, less than a month after voters backed an abortion-rights constitutional amendment. Jackson County Circuit Judge Jerri Zhang did not immediately rule after the hearing.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump picks Monica Crowley for senior State Department post
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday nominated Monica Crowley, a former Fox News contributor who faced public scrutiny during Trump’s first term over allegations of plagiarism, for a top public relations post in the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names Kelly Loeffler for SBA
item.description: The former Georgia lawmaker, who served about a year in the Senate starting in January 2020, also has been overseeing the incoming president’s inaugural committee with Steve Witkoff.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Two children, ages 5 and 6, wounded in shooting at California school; suspect dead
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Memphis police use excessive force and discriminate against Black people, Justice Department finds
item.description: The Memphis Police Department uses excessive force and discriminates against Black people, according to the findings of a U.S. Department of Justice investigation launched after the beating death of Tyre Nichols after a traffic stop in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Sainz And Jonathan Mattise, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jury revisits key videos in NYC subway chokehold death trial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Jurors asked Wednesday to review police and bystander video at the heart of the chokehold manslaughter case against Daniel Penny as his lawyers complained that the Marine veteran was being harassed outside the New York City courthouse.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Key things to know about the fatal attack on the UnitedHealthcare CEO in NYC
item.description: The chief executive of UnitedHealthcare, one of the nation's largest insurers, was killed Wednesday in midtown Manhattan in what police described as a targeted attack by a shooter outside a hotel where the company was holding a conference.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Searchers don't expect to find woman in sinkhole alive: Pennsylvania police
item.description: “We’ve had no signs of any form of life or anything,” Pennsylvania State Police spokesperson Trooper Steve Limani said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump changes White House counsel pick, tapping David Warrington
item.description: Trump in a statement said Warrington would serve as the top attorney in the White House and as assistant to the president.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Underground voids from abandoned mines pose danger to people and property when land collapses
item.description: Swaths of Pennsylvania and many other states are honeycombed with old, unstable mines that can cause the earth to suddenly give way.
item.dc:creator: Michael Rubinkam And Matthew Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold Tennessee's ban on medical treatments for transgender minors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hearing a high-profile culture-war clash, the Supreme Court on Wednesday seemed likely to uphold Tennessee's ban on gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Union rights take center stage in already high-stakes Wisconsin Supreme Court race
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin's state Supreme Court election next spring already had high stakes, with majority control on the line.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ohio school to offer new religious release program through Satanic Temple
item.description: A new religious release program for students is entering public schools in Marysville, Ohio, and this one emphasizes Satanic studies.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, Fox 5

item.title: Stadium-size asteroid to make closest approach to Earth
item.description: The stadium-size asteroid is considered a "potentially hazardous object," but poses no threat to Earth this year.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Mother fights for answers after Army son's mysterious death
item.description: Anna Stanley's 23-year-old son Quandarius was seriously hurt while delivering aid on an American-built pier in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Annette Peagler, NewsNation

item.title: California farmers are hopeful Trump administration will deliver more water to fields
item.description: Since winning the election, President-elect Donald Trump has been talking about immigration, border security and government efficiency. But in California farm country, his comments about water are also getting top attention.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump’s pick to run the DEA withdraws his name from consideration. Trump says he pulled the plug
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Chad Chronister, Donald Trump's pick to run the Drug Enforcement Administration, has withdrawn his name from consideration for the job, becoming the second person selected by the president-elect to bow out quickly after being selected for a position requiring Senate confirmation.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Shoplifter inspires new clothing line from Southern California boutique
item.description: After a Southern California store was targeted by a shoplifter, the owner decided to feature the thief as the star of his latest clothing collection.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KRON4

item.title: Lakefront family massacre teen suspect's attorneys show possible defense, claim forensics show innocence
item.description: Attorneys for a 15-year-old accused of slaughtering his parents and siblings in their Washington state lakeside house have defended the teenager's narrative of what happened.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Surveillance video shows Cash App founder using knife to snort cocaine before death, defense alleges
item.description: Lawyers for Nima Momeni, who is accused of killing Cash App founder Bob Lee, played a new surveillance video during their closing arguments in his murder trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US border arrests drop 17% in November, dousing predictions of post-election surge
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — U.S. authorities made about 46,700 arrests for illegally crossing the border from Mexico in November, down about 17% from October to a new low for Joe Biden's presidency, an official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Freedom is permanent for Missourian described as the longest-held wrongly incarcerated woman in US
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Years of legal wrangling have come to an end for a woman who spent 43 years behind bars for a killing that her attorneys argue was committed by a discredited police officer.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 8-year-old cancer survivor pushed down at Sunday's Bills-49ers game
item.description: The fun night was cut short after the family said a drunk Bills fan pushed Mia down the stairs in unshoveled section 312.
item.dc:creator: Dillon Morello, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Train slams into stuck semi-truck in Pennsylvania
item.description: City police believe the truck got hung up on the snow and the driver couldn't pull away before having to exit the cab before the train struck.
item.dc:creator: Bill Palmer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Police say a Hawaii woman disappeared voluntarily and traveled to Mexico
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A Hawaii woman who vanished after landing in Los Angeles three weeks ago disappeared voluntarily as she sought to “step away from modern connectivity” and was last seen crossing into Mexico with her luggage, police said at a news conference where they urged her to contact her dist
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, WGN 9
